---
title: 'From a different point of view, suffering may not be as bad we thought.'
date: '2020-02-17T15:42:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: false
categories:
  - 'Musings'
---

<div>The story is told of Norman Cousins, who developed a rare condition called ankylosing spondylitis, which is a degeneration of connective tissue in the spine. Essentially the patient becomes gradually more and more paralyzed. Creeping paralysis.</div><div><br></div><div>Among the things he discovered was the patient's loss of control over his own life and body was more damaging than the disease itself.</div><div><br></div><div>Philip Yancey tells us that when we do things like offering to take out the trash because that person is sick and shouldn't have to do it, or telling them to sit down while we do things for them, we actually take away some of their purpose and dignity and make it harder for them to recover, or at least enjoy end of life.</div><div><br></div><div>He talked about writing greeting cards that will celebrate the fact that they're still alive and that parts of their body are still working, instead of cards pointing out how much they're missing out on and urging them to get well.</div><div><br></div><div>Norman Cousins moved himself to a hotel room so that he could have control over his own schedule. Doctors had predicted at least partial paralysis and yet between the hotel room and watching lots of funny movies which encouraged his body to heal through laughter he made a full recovery.</div><div><br></div><div>So this has me wondering...</div><div><br></div><div>How many of us are perfectly healthy physically but incapacitated mentally and emotionally because of our own attitudes and outlooks?</div><div><br></div><div>Often our perception of reality is more important than the reality.</div><div><br></div><div>If something in your life, be it a relationship, an idea, a job, a deeply held belief... Or even a meme on Facebook... Is adding stress to your emotional state, if it is not bringing peace joy and love... Maybe it's time to evaluate whether that thing is worth having around.</div><div><br></div><div>You have a finite amount of energy to give this world. Some of that energy can be recharged with positivity. So that energy can be stolen with negativity.</div><div><br></div><div>What are you going to do with the 24 hours you have today?</div><div><br></div><div>How would changing your outlook and perspective and focus, change your experience?</div><div><br></div><div>Selah</div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;">
  <a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1581954117285187-0-867f7c293cb0.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;">
    <img border="0"   src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1581954117285187-0-867f7c293cb0.png" width="400">
  </a>
</div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;">
  <a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1581954089009639-1-b2c4d400ffcf.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;">
    <img border="0"   src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1581954089009639-1-b2c4d400ffcf.png" width="400">
  </a>
</div><br></div>
